_____ side, the marriage is prohibited.
Paternal (Pitripaksh)
397
If a bride's 'Chhatth' appears in the groom's _____ side, the 
marriage is permitted (after the 5th generation).
Maternal (Matripaksh)
398
What is 'Dushana Panji'?
A genealogical record that specifically tracks any flaws or historical 
transgressions in a family's lineage.
399
What is the difference between 'Mool' and 'Moolgram'?
Mool is the original ancestral village, while Moolgram is the 
specific village where the lineage resided during the Panji's 
formalisation.
400
Which Panjikar was initially appointed for the Brahmin community 
by King Harisimhadeva?
Gunaikar Jha.
401
Which Panjikar was initially appointed for the Karn Kayastha 
community?
Shankardatta.
402
Which Panjikar was initially appointed for the Kshatriya 
community?
Vijayadatta.
403
What is the significance of the month of 'Leo' (Sinha Rashi) for 
Panji preservation?
It is the recommended time to dry and air out the Panji manuscripts 
to protect them from moisture and pests.
404
The classification 'Pachhwari-paat' refers to which group of Maithil 
Brahmins?
The non-Shrotriya Brahmins (Yogya and Panjibaddh).
405
The classification 'Purbari-paat' refers to which group of Maithil 
Brahmins?
The Shrotriya Brahmins.
406
Which Sanskrit work provided the verse detailing the Saka year 
1019 as the foundation for Nanyadeva's rule in Mithila?
The introductory verses of the Panji records.
407
Name one of the seven primary, most populous gotras of Maithil 
Brahmins.
Sandilya (others include Vatsa, Kashyap, Savarna, Parashara, 
Bharadwaj, and Katyayan).
408
According to the rule 'Matritah panchami, pitritah saptami', how 
many maternal generations must be avoided?
Five generations.
409
In the calculation of marriage eligibility, the bride must not be the 
daughter of the groom's _____.
Maternal uncle (Maima).
410
Who was the author of 'Smritisar', whose personal genealogical 
error led to the Panji reform?
Pandit Harinath Upadhyay.
411
Term: Shakha Panji
Definition: A specialized genealogical book documenting the 
branches and status changes of families, particularly Shrotriyas.
412
How many categories of Shrotriya Brahmins are currently identified 
in the records?
Eight (known as Chauwala or Chaurayil).
413
How many Shrotriya Panjis are currently in existence?
32
414
How many Panjis exist for the Yogya and Panjibaddh categories 
combined?
153

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
415
Concept: Adhogami
Definition: The downward social mobility of a lineage's status due 
to marriages into lower-ranked families.
416
In the list of 32 ancestral roots (Uteh), the 5th 'Chhatth' refers to the 
bride's _____.
Paternal grandmother's great-grandfather.
417
Which scripts were traditionally used to write the Panji records?
Tirhuta (Mithilakshar).
418
Why is the Savarna gotra prohibited from marrying into the Vatsa 
gotra?
Because they share identical 'Pravaras' (sage ancestors).
419
Which specific ancestor is the 'Bijipurush' of the Simhashram Mool? Mahamahopadhyaya Halayudha.
420
The 10th 'Chhatth' in the genealogical search refers to the bride's 
_____.
Great-grandfather's maternal grandfather (Pramatamaha).
421
Under whose reign did the 'Shakha Panji' and the rigid hierarchy of 
Shrotriyas begin?
Maharaja Madhav Singh.
422
Which type of Panji record tracks historical ' Kshatran' or specific 
family status changes?
Dushana Panji.
423
The terminology 'Laukik' is primarily associated with which group's 
genealogy?
Shrotriya Brahmins.
424
What is the 'Aswajan-yapar' Panji?
A record detailing descendants from inter-caste or prohibited 
marriages.
425
In the context of Panji, what does 'Chhatth' literally mean in the 
calculation of prohibited degrees?
The sixth position/generation in a specific genealogical line.
426
Which Sanskrit work is cited to define the Sapinda relationship as 
'five from the mother and seven from the father'?
Manusmriti.
427
The Panji records identify Maithils following the Vajasaneyi branch 
as followers of which Veda?
Yajurveda.
428
Which specific scholar's lineage is traced back to Tripurari Bhatta 
(c. 400 AD) in the Panji records?
Aryabhatta.
429
The primary duty of a Panjikar before a marriage is to verify that 
the couple are _____.
Asapinda (not related within prohibited degrees).
430
In what year was the Panji Prabandh system established in Mithila 
according to the source?
$1326$ AD (Saka $1248$).
431
Which Karnata dynasty ruler is credited with the formalisation of 
the Maithil Panji system?
Maharaja Harisimhadeva.
432
The Maithil Panji system was primarily created to maintain the 
purity of which three castes?
Brahmin, Kayastha, and Kshatriya.
433
What is the term for the introduction of seven generations of 
ancestors required for marriage eligibility?
Udhedh.
434
According to the Panji system, how many maternal ancestors must 
be introduced for an 'Udhedh'?
Five generations.
435
According to the Panji system, how many paternal ancestors must 
be introduced for an 'Udhedh'?
Seven generations.
436
Which Maithil Brahmin group is designated as 'Jaywar'?
Those who were not registered in the formal Panji records.
437
In the modern Panji system, what is the required generation gap to 
be discarded on the father's side for marriage?
Seven generations (modified to six in later practices).
438
In the modern Panji system, what is the required generation gap to 
be discarded on the mother's side for marriage?
Five generations.
439
The Maithil Brahmins are divided into how many 'Mools' (original 
ancestral villages)?
$167$ Mools.
440
How many main 'Gotras' are listed for Maithil Brahmins in total?
$20$ Gotras.
441
Identify the two main Vedic branches (Shakhas) followed by 
Maithil Brahmins.
Yajurveda (Madhyandina) and Samaveda (Kauthuma).
442
Followers of the Samaveda Kauthuma branch are specifically 
referred to as _____.
Chhandog.
443
Followers of the Yajurveda Madhyandina branch are specifically 
referred to as _____.
Vajasaneyi.
444
What is the primary difference in the marriage ceremony of 
Chhandog Maithil Brahmins compared to others?
It is divided into two parts: Purva-Vivah and Uttar-Vivah (after 
seeing the star Arundhati).
445
What defines a 'Tri-pravara' family in the Maithil Brahmin tradition? They have three ancestors who composed Rigvedic hymns.
446
What defines a 'Panch-pravara' family in the Maithil Brahmin 
tradition?
They have five ancestors who composed Rigvedic hymns.
447
Which two Gotras are forbidden from intermarrying because they 
share the same ancestors?
Vatsa and Savarna.
448
Who was the scholar whose 'Smritisara' text highlighted the danger 
of unintentional incestuous marriage, leading to the Panji system?
Pandit Harinath Upadhyaya.
449
The professional genealogist appointed by the King to maintain 
lineage records is known as a _____.
Panjikar.

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
450
Which individual was first tasked by King Harisimhadeva to 
manage the Brahmin Panji records?
Gunakar Jha.
451
Which individual was first tasked by King Harisimhadeva to 
manage the Karna Kayastha Panji records?
Shankardatta.
452
Which individual was first tasked by King Harisimhadeva to 
manage the Kshatriya Panji records?
Vijaydatta.
453
Identify the four social hierarchical categories of Maithil Brahmins 
created after the Panji management by Maharaja Madhav Singh.
Shrotriya, Yogya, Panjibaddh, and Jaywar.
454
The 'Panch-Gaud' classification includes Brahmins from Utkal, 
Kanyakubja, Gauda, Saraswat, and _____.
Maithil.
455
In the Panji system, what does 'Biji Purush' represent?
The earliest known progenitor or ancestor of a specific lineage.
456
Concept: Chhatwidh
Definition: Sixteen specific ancestral connections (e.g. Great-
grandfather's maternal grandfather) checked to ensure marriage 
eligibility.
457
According to the source, why is the Panji system compared to 
'Genome Mapping'?
Because it scientifically tracks genetic lineage to prevent 
consanguinity (inbreeding).
458
What is the script traditionally used to write the Panji records?
Tirhuta (Mithilakshar).
459
Which philosopher mentioned 'Samuha-lekhya' (group records) in 
his 'Tantravartika' as a precursor to lineage tracking?
Kumarila Bhatta.
460
How many ancestors are included in the 'Yavro Parichay' (thirty-
two Mools introduction) during marriage rituals?
Thirty-two.
461
The practice of 'Dushan Panji' involves recording what type of 
information?
The faults or 'kshat' (blemishes) that occurred in a lineage over time.
462
Which Maithil King initiated the 'Shakha Panji' (branch records) 
which led to further social stratification?
Maharaja Madhav Singh.
463
In the Karna Kayastha community, how many Gotras exist?
Only one (Kashyap).
464
According to the rules of 'Sapinda', a marriage is generally avoided 
if the bride is within _____ generations of the groom's mother.
Five generations.
465
The specific village where a 'Biji Purush' originally resided is called 
the _____.
Moolgram.
466
What is the status of the Panji system in the Maithil Rajput 
(Kshatriya) community today according to the source?
It has largely disappeared, though some rules regarding village 
avoidance remain.
467
The term 'Asvajana Patra' is a synonym for which other document?
Siddhanta Patra.
468
According to the mnemonic 'Jalat Rakshet, Tailat Rakshet', what 
should the Panji manuscripts be protected from?
Water, oil, and loose binding.
469
During which solar month is it recommended to dry Panji 
manuscripts in the sun to prevent decay?
When the sun is in the sign of Leo (mid-August to mid-September).
470
Which Gotra is associated with the 'Khandabala' Mool, from which 
the Kings of Darbhanga emerged?
Shandilya.
471
What was the significance of the year $450$ AD in the 'Panji 
Theory Merged' text?
It marks the earliest ancestral starting point for the lineage data 
contained in the records.
472
Which social class of Maithil Brahmins is traditionally referred to 
as 'Pachhwari Paat'?
Non-Shrotriya Brahmins (Yogya and Panjibaddh).
473
Which social class of Maithil Brahmins is traditionally referred to 
as 'Pubari Paat'?
Shrotriya Brahmins.
474
The classification of Brahmins into ten types (Five Gauda, Five 
Dravida) was formalised at which geographical boundary?
The Vindhya Mountains.
475
A marriage resulting in a 'Chandala' child is defined in Smritis as a 
marriage with a _____.
Swajana (closely related relative).
476
Which deity or sage's name is often identical to the name of a 
'Gotra' in Maithil tradition?
The ancient Rishi who founded the lineage.
477
The first 'Chhatwidh' ancestor to be checked for a bride is her 
_____.
Paternal Great-great-grandfather.
478
What is the title of the work by Jyotirishwar Thakur that mentions 
King Harisimhadeva?
Varna Ratnakar.
479
What is the purpose of 'Utekt' in the context of marriage?
It is a list of potential grooms for a girl, verified by the Panjikar $5$-
$10$ years before marriage.
480
Identify the primary ethical concern raised by the author regarding 
modern Panjikars.
The commercialisation of the profession and charging excessive 
fees for services.
481
According to the text, the Panji system was a 'scientific basis' for 
what specific type of introduction?
Vamshavali (Genealogical) identity.
482
What was the primary purpose of the 'Panji Prabandh' (genealogical 
management) system when it was created in the medieval period?
To ensure 'Jati Shuddhita' (caste purity) for Brahmins, Kayasthas, 
and Kshatriyas.

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
483
The Panji system requires an introduction of how many generations 
for a Brahmin marriage candidate, a process known as 'Uterh'?
Seven generations.
484
To establish an individual's 'Uterh', how many specific family 
backgrounds (Kula) must be introduced?
Thirty-two.
485
Into which three primary categories are Maithil Brahmins divided 
according to the Panji system?
Srotriya, Yogya, and Panjibaddh.
486
What term is used to describe individuals who were not registered 
in the Panji records?
Jaywar.
487
In the Srotriya category, how many maternal generations must be 
avoided for a marriage to be permissible?
Five generations.
488
In the Srotriya category, how many paternal generations must be 
avoided for a valid marriage?
Seven generations.
489
For the 'Panjibaddh' category, how many paternal generations must 
be avoided in a marriage union?
Six generations.
490
How many 'Gotras' (lineages) are recorded within the Maithil 
Brahmin community?
Twenty.
491
How many 'Mula Grams' (original ancestral villages) were initially 
identified in the Panji records?
One hundred and sixty-seven.
492
How many primary 'Mulas' (roots) were determined out of the total 
167?
Thirty-four (later thirty-five with the addition of the 'Khama' 
branch).
493
Based on Vedic rituals, Maithil Brahmins are divided into which 
two groups?
Chhandog (Samavedic) and Vajasaneyi (Yajurvedic).
494
According to the Puranas, the ten divisions of Brahmins are broadly 
categorised as 'Pancha Gaur' and _____.
Pancha Dravid.
495
Which geographical feature serves as the dividing line between the 
Pancha Gaur and Pancha Dravid Brahmins?
The Vindhyachal Mountains.
496
Maithil Brahmins belong to which of the two broad Puranic groups 
of Indian Brahmins?
Pancha Gaur.
497
Vajasaneyi Brahmins follow which branch of the Yajurveda?
The Madhyandina branch.
498
Chhandog Brahmins follow which branch of the Samaveda?
The Kauthum branch.
499
In Chhandog marriages, when must the 'Uttar Vivah' (latter 
marriage) ideally take place?
After sunset, following the sighting of the star Arundhati.
500
According to the texts of Manu and Yajnavalkya, a marriage is 
forbidden if the bride is a 'Sapinda' of which two relatives?
The mother and the father.
501
What is the 'Samuha Lekhya', first mentioned by Kumarila Bhatta 
in 'Tantra Varttik'?
A written family record used to protect lineage and track virtues or 
defects.
502
What historical event in 1326 AD (Shaka 1248) led to the formal 
institutionalisation of the Panji records?
The decision by King Harisimhadeva to standardise genealogical 
records under state-appointed Pandits.
503
What is the title of a state-appointed Pandit authorised to verify 
marriage eligibility in Mithila?
Panjikar.
504
What is the 'Siddhanta Patra' (or Aswajanaya Patra) in the context 
of Maithil marriage?
A certificate of eligibility issued by a Panjikar confirming the bride 
and groom are not closely related.
505
In the Panji system, what does the term 'Beeji Purush' represent?
The earliest known ancestor or founder of a specific lineage/Kula.
506
The 'Mandav' village is the original home of a major Kula in which 
Gotra?
Kashyap.
507
In the Maithil Brahmin context, what does a 'Gotra' signify?
The name of a remote, learned ancestor (Rishi) from whom the 
family descends paternally.
508
Which seven Gotras are considered most prominent and populous 
among Maithil Brahmins?
Shandilya, Vatsa, Kashyap, Savarna, Parashar, Bharadwaj, and 
Katyayan.
509
What is a 'Pravara'?
A list of distinguished Vedic ancestors (Rishis) associated with a 
specific Gotra.
510
How are 'Tri-pravara' and 'Panch-pravara' lineages distinguished?
By whether three or five of their ancestors composed hymns 
(Suktams) for the Rigveda.
511
Marriage is prohibited between the Vatsa and Savarna Gotras 
because they share what commonality?
They share the same ancestors (lineage).
512
Who was the first person assigned by King Harisimhadeva to 
manage the Brahmin Panji records?
Gunakar Jha.
513
Who was the first person assigned to manage the Karna Kayastha 
Panji records?
Shankardatta.
514
Who was the first person assigned to manage the Kshatriya Panji 
records under King Harisimhadeva?
Vijaydatta.
515
Which Maithil King introduced 'Shakha Panji' and the hierarchy of 
Srotriya classification?
Maharaja Madhav Singh.
516
What single Gotra is found among the Karna Kayastha community? Kashyap.
517
The term 'Pubaripara' refers to which class of Maithil Brahmins?
Srotriyas.

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
518
The term 'Pachhbaripara' refers to which class of Maithil Brahmins? The remaining Brahmins (Yogya, Panjibaddh, etc.) excluding 
Srotriyas.
519
According to the Panji preservation rule, from what three things 
must a Panji book be protected?
Water, oil, and loose binding.
520
What is 'Adhikar Mala'?
A list of potential eligible grooms prepared by a Panjikar for a 
bride's father years before marriage.
521
How many specific 'Chhatth' (genealogical relationship nodes) are 
checked to determine marriage eligibility?
Sixteen.
522
In the Panji system, what is the 'Dushan Panji' used for?
To record any flaws or 'Kshayan' (decay) that occurred in a specific 
lineage.
523
Term: Sapinda
Definition: Blood relatives sharing the same body particles, within 
specified generational limits for marriage exclusion.
524
Marriage is prohibited if the groom is a descendant of the bride's 
maternal _____.
Uncle (Mama).
525
Which Pandit of the Pandua Mula was the very first 'Panji 
Prabandhak' appointed by the King?
Ratnakar Jha.
526
In the Panji script, what is the 'Tirhuta' script?
The traditional script of Mithila in which Panji records were 
historically maintained.
527
Concept: Mula Panji
Definition: The original records containing the comprehensive 
genealogical data of all families within a group.
528
Which Puranic list identifies Utkal, Kanyakubja, Gauda, Maithil, 
and Saraswat as the _____ Gaur group?
Pancha.
529
Which Puranic list identifies Karnat, Gurjar, Tailang, Dravid, and 
Maharashtra as the _____ Dravid group?
Pancha.
530
In 'Uterh' calculations, the 1st 'Chhatth' refers to the bride's _____.
Vriddha Prapitamaha (Great-great-grandfather).
531
The 2nd 'Chhatth' in marriage eligibility refers to the bride's _____.
Prapitamaha's Matamaha (Great-grandfather's maternal grandfather).
532
According to the rules of Sapinda, how many generations are 
avoided in the maternal line in the Smritis?
Five generations.
533
According to the Smritis, how many generations are avoided in the 
paternal line?
Seven generations.
534
If a bride belongs to any of the 16 Chhatth nodes of the groom's 
paternal side, the marriage is _____.
Prohibited (Invalid).
535
If a bride belongs to a Chhatth node on the groom's maternal side, is 
the marriage generally permitted?
Yes, it is often considered permissible ('Adhikar ho jayet').
536
Which famous Maithil scholar and king-priest is mentioned as 
having a lineage detailed over 19 generations in the Panji?
Mahamahopadhyaya Mahesh Thakur.
537
The Panji records for the Karna Kayastha community differ from 
Brahmins because they primarily use _____ for Uterh.
Mula (Roots) instead of Gotra.
538
In Panji preservation, which month is considered best for sun-
drying the manuscripts due to intense heat?
Bhadrapada (approx. 16 August to 15 September).
539
A marriage where the couple shares the same Gotra or Pravara is 
considered _____.
Ashastriya (against scriptures) and prohibited.
540
What is the status of a child born from a 'Swajana' (related) 
marriage according to ancient Smritis?
They were historically considered 'Chandal' and excluded from 
Aryan dignity.
541
Which Vedic sage's hymns are associated with the 'Shandilya' Gotra 
for Pravara patha?
Shandilya, Asit, and Deval.
542
The Vatsa Gotra includes which five Rishis in its Panch-pravara?
Aurva, Chyavan, Bhargava, Jamadagnya, and Apnavan.
543
The Bharadwaj Gotra includes which three Rishis in its Tri-pravara? Bharadwaj, Angiras, and Barhaspatya.
544
Which Panji type specifically tracks the maternal grandfather's root 
and village for the groom?
Pan Panji.
545
The 'Khama' branch of the Phandah Mula is notable for what 
historical reason?
It was established as the 35th Mula for a high lineage in a later 
period.
546
What historical source provides descriptions of 72 Rajput clans, 
some of which also used Panji records?
Varna-Ratnakar by Jyotirishwar Thakur.
547
Historically, which three social classes in Mithila maintained Panji 
records?
Brahmin, Kayastha, and Kshatriya.
548
Which Maithil scholar of the 18th generation from Gunakar Jha 
was awarded the title 'Panji Shastra Martand'?
Modanand Jha.
549
The 'Laukik' system is specifically associated with the Panjis of 
which group?
Srotriya Brahmins.
550
How many 'Chauvala' (classes) exist within the Srotriya Panji 
hierarchy?
Eight.
551
What is a 'Beeji Purush'?
The founder-ancestor from whom all branches of a Mula originate.
552
The 'Mula Gram' represents the _____ of a particular family branch. Original ancestral residence.

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
553
In the Panji system, the term 'Ayanstha Shreshtha' refers to which 
category of roots?
The first/top category of ancestral roots.
554
Identify one root (Mula) from the 'Madhyam Mula' (middle 
category).
Dighway (or Belaunch, Ekahate, Panchobhe, etc.).
555
The 'Kauthum' branch belongs to which Veda?
Samaveda.
556
Which Veda's followers are called 'Vajasaneyi'?
Yajurveda.
557
According to the Panji theory, 'Jati' is determined by what factor?
Purity of birth (Janma-shuddhita).
558
Which historical work by Jyotirishwar mentions the existence of 
Panjis for the 'Ran-dhariya' Rajputs?
Varna-Ratnakar.
559
What is the meaning of 'Asanpinda' in the context of marriage?
A person who does not share a common ancestor within the 
forbidden generational range.
560
If a Panjikar finds a lineage flaw in the groom's family, in which 
Panji is this recorded?
Dushan Panji.
561
Which three social groups were originally included in the Panji 
Prabandh during the medieval period?
Brahmins, Kayasthas, and Kshatriyas.
562
In the context of Maithil genealogy, what does the term 'Uderh' 
refer to?
The introduction or identification of seven generations of ancestors.
563
How many total ancestral lines (Kulas) are required for a complete 
'Uderh' identification?
Thirty-two.
564
Into which three hierarchical categories were Maithil Brahmins 
divided following the Panji registration?
Shrotriya, Yogya, and Panjibaddh.
565
What was the status of Brahmins who were not registered in the 
official Panji records?
They were known as Jaibar.
566
According to the Shrotriya tradition, how many generations must be 
avoided on the mother's side for marriage?
Five generations.
567
According to the Shrotriya tradition, how many generations must be 
avoided on the father's side for marriage?
Seven generations.
568
How many distinct 'Gotras' are recognized among Maithil 
Brahmins?
Twenty.
569
How many 'Moolgrams' (ancestral villages) were identified during 
the initial Panji Prabandh?
One hundred and sixty-seven.
570
In the Maithil Panji system, what is a 'Biji Purush'?
The earliest known progenitor or founding ancestor of a specific 
lineage (Kula).
571
Which Maithil Brahmin Gotras are classified as 'Panch-pravara' 
(having five ancestors who composed Vedic hymns)?
Vatsa, Savarna, and Bharadwaj (though Bharadwaj is often Tri-
pravara, text notes Vatsa, Savarna, and Bharv as Panch-pravara).
572
What distinguishes a 'Tri-pravara' Gotra from a 'Panch-pravara' 
Gotra?
Tri-pravara identifies three sage ancestors, while Panch-pravara 
identifies five.
573
Why is marriage prohibited between the Vatsa and Savarna Gotras 
despite being distinct?
They share the same common ancestors.
574
Maithil Brahmins belong to which broader classification of Indian 
Brahmins?
Panch Gaur.
575
What geographical feature serves as the boundary between the 
'Panch Gaur' and 'Panch Dravid' Brahmin groups?
The Vindhyachal Mountains.
576
Maithil Brahmins follow which two specific Vedas?
Yajurveda and Samaveda.
577
Followers of the Madhyandini branch of the Yajurveda are known 
as _____.
Vajasaneyi.
578
Followers of the Kauthuma branch of the Samaveda are known as 
_____.
Chandoga.
579
Which historical King of Mithila is credited with formalising the 
Panji system in 1326 AD?
Maharaja Harisimhadeva.
580
What specific incident involving Pundit Harinath Upadhyaya led to 
the systematisation of Panji records?
He inadvertently married a girl who was his first cousin (maternal 
uncle's daughter).
581
What is the official title of the scholars appointed by the state to 
maintain and verify genealogical records?
Panjikar.
582
What is a 'Siddhanta Patra' (also known as Asvajana Patra)?
A document issued by a Panjikar certifying that a couple is not 
related and is eligible to marry.
583
In Maithil genealogy, what is the difference between a 'Mool' and a 
'Moolgram'?
Mool is the original lineage name, while Moolgram is the specific 
village associated with that lineage.
584
The first 'Panjikar' appointed for the Brahmin community by King 
Harisimhadeva was _____.
Gunakar Jha.
585
Who was appointed as the first Panjikar for the Karna Kayastha 
community?
Shankaradatta.
586
Under which ruler did the 'Shakha Panji' (branch records) begin to 
create finer sub-divisions like Shrotriya?
Maharaja Madhav Singh.
587
What does 'Dushan Panji' record?
The flaws or social transgressions associated with specific lineages.
588
The 'Chhavith' rule in Mithila marriage eligibility refers to checking 
_____ specific ancestral lines of the bride and groom.
Sixteen.

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
589
How many generations are avoided for marriage on the father's side 
in the 'Panjibaddh' category?
Six generations.
590
Term: Svajana
Definition: A term used for relatives within the prohibited degrees 
of marriage; marrying a Svajana was historically considered a 
major transgression.
591
Which script was traditionally used to write and preserve the Panji 
records?
Tirhuta (Mithilakshar).
592
How many primary 'Mools' were determined to be of the highest 
rank (Ayanti Shrestha)?
Thirteen.
593
Process: Marriage Eligibility
Step: Verify that the bride and groom do not share the same Gotra 
or Pravara.
594
Which religious text serves as the foundation for the marriage rules 
used in the Panji system?
Dharmashastras (specifically Smritis like Manu and Yajnavalkya).
595
What is the significance of the 15th-century philosopher Kumarila 
Bhatta in Panji history?
He mentioned 'Samuha Lekhya', an early form of collective lineage 
records.
596
According to the text, which month is considered ideal for drying 
and preserving Panji manuscripts?
Bhadrapada (when the Sun is in Leo), due to the intense heat.
597
Concept: Samreydi Brahmins
Attribute: They are the followers of the Samaveda and are also 
called Chandogas.
598
Concept: Sukla Yajurvedi Brahmins
Attribute: They are the followers of the Yajurveda and are also 
called Vajasaneyis.
599
How does the Upanayana (thread ceremony) of a Chandoga differ 
from a Vajasaneyi?
Chandogas have four specific samskaras (naming, tonsure, 
initiation, graduation), while Vajasaneyis include Vedarambha 
(starting Vedic study).
600
What is the major difference in marriage rituals between Chandoga 
and Vajasaneyi Brahmins?
Chandoga marriages are divided into two parts (Purva and Uttara), 
whereas Vajasaneyi marriages are performed in a single sequence.
601
In the Panji system, what is the 'Uteht' of a groom?
The detailed genealogical record of the groom's 32 ancestral lines 
used to check marriage eligibility.
602
According to the rules of 'Chhavith', a groom's mother's side must 
be clear of the bride's line for at least _____ generations.
Five.
603
The term 'Pachhwari Paith' is used to refer to which group of 
Maithil Brahmins?
The Non-Shrotriya Brahmins (Yogya and Panjibaddh).
604
What is the 'Laukik' in the context of Shrotriya Panji?
The specific village-based social identity or 'branch' within the 
Shrotriya hierarchy.
605
Which Gotra has the highest number of branches (seventy-four) 
mentioned in the text?
Kashyap.
606
According to the text, what is the status of a child born from a 
'Svajana' (prohibited) marriage?
The child was historically considered a 'Chandal' and excluded from 
Aryan social limits.
607
Which specific Gotra is the only one found among Karna 
Kayasthas, according to the Panji records?
Kashyap.
608
The 'Panch Gaur' category includes Saraswat, Kanyakubja, Gaur, 
Maithil, and _____ Brahmins.
Utkal.
609
The 'Panch Dravid' category includes Karnat, Gurjar, Tailang, 
Dravid, and _____ Brahmins.
Maharashtriya.
610
Who was the legendary scholar known for the 'Smritisara' whose 
marriage mistake triggered the formalisation of Panji?
Pundit Harinath Upadhyaya.
611
In Panji terminology, what does 'Panjibaddh' literally mean?
One whose name and lineage are officially entered into the Panji 
records.
612
Which category of 'Mool' includes Dighve, Belaonchai, and 
Ekahate?
Madhyam Mool (Medium Mools).
613
A marriage is prohibited if the bride is the daughter of the groom's 
_____.
Maternal uncle (mother's brother).
614
What is the primary function of the 'Siddhanta' ceremony in Maithil 
Brahmin culture?
The public verification of genealogical eligibility before a marriage 
is finalised.
615
Which historical figure is described as the 'Karnat Chudamani' 
(Jewel of the Karnat dynasty)?
Maharaja Harisimhadeva.
616
What is the 'Adhikar Mala' in the Panji system?
A list of potential eligible grooms prepared for a girl several years 
before her marriage.
617
How many hierarchical 'Chauras' (categories) exist within the 
Shrotriya Panji?
Eight.
618
In the Panji system, why is the record of 'Dushan' (flaws) 
maintained?
To ensure that future generations remain aware of ancestral lapses 
and avoid their consequences.
619
Which ruler is credited with the formal systematisation of the Panji 
Prabandh in Mithila in 1326 AD?
Maharaja Harisimhadeva.
620
What event served as the primary catalyst for King Harisimhadeva 
to formalise genealogical records?
Pandit Harinath Upadhyay accidentally married his first cousin due 
to a lack of recorded family history.
621
Term: Uderh
Definition: The genealogical pedigree consisting of 32 ancestral 
lines required to verify marriage eligibility.

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
622
The formalisation of the Panji system in Mithila took place in the 
year $1248$ of which era?
The Shaka Era (corresponding to $1326$ AD).
623
What is the status of a child born from a 'Swa-jana' marriage 
(marriage within prohibited degrees)?
The child is traditionally considered a 'Chandala' and the family 
loses its social dignity.
624
Maithil Brahmins primarily belong to which of the five northern 
Brahmin groups?
The Panch-Gaur group.
625
Which mountain range serves as the traditional geographic 
boundary between the Panch-Gaur and Panch-Dravida Brahmins?
The Vindhyachal mountains.
626
How many distinct Gotras are traditionally recognised within the 
Maithil Brahmin community?
$20$.
627
Term: Mula-gram
Definition: The original ancestral village or home of a family 
lineage as recorded in the Panji.
628
In the Panji system, what is a 'Biji Purush'?
The earliest known founding ancestor from whom a specific family 
line is traced.
629
What distinguishes a 'Tri-pravar' Gotra from a 'Panch-pravar' Gotra? A Tri-pravar has three Rigvedic hymn-composing ancestors, while 
a Panch-pravar has five.
630
Why are marriages between members of the Vatsa and Savarna 
Gotras prohibited?
They share the same set of five ancestors (Panch-pravars), making 
them effectively the same lineage.
631
The Panji records identify a person's pedigree by tracing how many 
total ancestral roots?
$32$.
632
Term: Chhatth
Definition: The sixteen specific positions in the maternal and 
paternal lines used to determine 'Sapiá¹‡á¸ a' (prohibited 
relationship) status.
633
According to the Smritis followed in the Panji system, how many 
generations of paternal ancestry are usually prohibited for marriage?
Seven generations (though sometimes modified to six).
634
How many generations must be clear of a common ancestor on the 
maternal side for a Maithil marriage to be permitted?
Five generations.
635
What is the function of a 'Siddhant' or 'Aswajana-patra'?
It is a certificate issued by a Panjikar confirming that the bride and 
groom are not related within prohibited degrees.
636
Which scholar was appointed by King Harisimhadeva as the first 
official Panjikar for the Brahmin community?
Gunakar Jha of Pandua Mula.
637
Who was the first scholar appointed to maintain the Panji records 
for the Karna Kayastha community?
Shankardutt.
638
Which group of Kshatriyas in Mithila is specifically mentioned as 
having historically maintained Panji records?
The Gandhavariya Rajputs.
639
How many original 'Mula' (root villages) were initially identified for 
the Brahmin community during the Panji Prabandh?
$167$.
640
Maithil Brahmins who follow the Madhyandini Shakha of the 
Shukla Yajurveda are known as _____.
Vajasaneyis.
641
Maithil Brahmins who follow the Kauthuma branch of the 
Samaveda are known as _____.
Chhandogyas.
642
What is the primary script used to maintain traditional Panji 
genealogical records?
Tirhuta (also known as Mithilakshar).
643
According to the traditional verse, what are the three hazards Panji 
manuscripts must be protected from?
Water, oil, and loose binding.
644
During which solar month should Panji manuscripts be dried in the 
sun for preservation?
When the Sun is in Leo (Sinha Rashi), typically mid-August to mid-
September.
645
Which 7th-century philosopher first mentioned 'Samuh Lekhya' 
(collective genealogical records) in the work 'Tantravartika'?
Kumarila Bhatta.
646
How many Gotras are recognised among the Karna Kayasthas of 
Mithila?
One (Kashyap).
647
What hierarchical classification was introduced to the Panji system 
under Maharaja Madhav Singh (c. $1760$ AD)?
The classification into Shrotriya, Yogya, and Panjibaddha 
categories.
648
What is the primary ritual difference between Chhandogya and 
Vajasaneyi weddings in Mithila?
Chhandogyas must perform the Uttar-Vivah ceremony after sunset 
after sighting the star Arundhati.
649
Marriage is strictly prohibited with the offspring of which maternal 
relative?
The maternal uncle (MÄ ma).
650
Under the Panji rules, why is a person forbidden from marrying the 
child of their step-mother's brother?
They are considered a 'Swa-jana' (self-kin) relative under Smriti law.
651
What does the term 'Pachhwari' refer to in the context of Maithil 
Brahmin geography?
The group of Brahmins traditionally residing in the western part of 
the region.
652
How many Shrotriya Panjis are traditionally recognized?
$32$.
653
Term: Jaibar
Definition: Brahmins whose ancestors were not recorded in the 
initial Panji registers but are part of the broader community.
654
The 'Genome Mapping' mentioned in the source title is a modern 
pedagogical comparison for which traditional system?
The Panji Prabandh (Genealogical System) of Mithila.
655
Which two ancient Smriti texts are the primary authorities for the 
marriage laws within the Panji system?
Manu Smriti and Yajnavalkya Smriti.

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
656
What is the traditional name for the certificates confirming that a 
bride and groom do not share a prohibited common ancestor?
Siddhant Patra.
657
Which solar month is considered to have the most intense heat 
('Kardav') suitable for drying Panji palm leaves?
Sinha (Leo).
658
Concept: Tri-pravar Gotras
Example: Sandilya, Kashyap, and Parashar are examples of Gotras 
with three hymn-composing ancestors.
659
Concept: Panch-pravar Gotras
Example: Vatsa, Savarna, and Bhardwaj (in some contexts) are 
examples of Gotras with five hymn-composing ancestors.
660
Which specific branch of the Shukla Yajurveda is followed by the 
Vajasaneyi Brahmins of Mithila?
The Madhyandini Shakha.
661
The 10th-century division of Indian Brahmins into ten groups is 
categorized into which two major five-group blocks?
Panch-Gaur and Panch-Dravida.
662
Which historical ruler is associated with the Shaka year $1019$ in 
the preface of the Panji records?
King Nanyadeva.
663
Term: Laukik
Definition: The specific lineage or sub-group identifier used within 
the Shrotriya Panji records.
664
What is the duty of a Panjikar towards the future of the 
genealogical profession?
To train disciples (shishyas) to ensure the Tirhuta script and 
genealogical knowledge do not go extinct.
665
According to the source, why did separate Brahmin cultures emerge 
in regions like Mithila or Gaur?
Due to regional differences in climate, language, diet, attire, and 
geographic isolation.
666
What is the total number of Panjis dedicated to the Yogya and 
Panjibaddha classes?
$153$.
667
The ancient Videha dynasty was centered in which geographical 
region?
Mithila.
668
Who is credited with the systematic organisation of the 'Panji 
Prabandh' in Mithila?
Maharaja Harisimhadeva.
669
In what year was the 'Panji Prabandh' established according to the 
Shaka calendar?
$1248$ Shaka (approximately $1326$ AD).
670
What was the primary social objective of establishing the Panji 
system?
To maintain 'Jati Shuddhi' (caste purity) and record authentic 
lineages for marriage.
671
In Maithil Brahmin genealogy, what does the term 'Udedh' signify?
The detailed introduction and identification of $32$ ancestors.
672
According to the marriage laws recorded, how many generations 
must be avoided on the paternal side?
Seven generations.
673
According to the marriage laws recorded, how many generations 
must be avoided on the maternal side?
Five generations.
674
What are the three main hierarchical tiers of Maithil Brahmins 
established by the Panji system?
Shrotriya, Yogya, and Panjibaddha.
675
What term is used for Brahmins who were not registered in the 
formal Panji system?
Jaibar.
676
How many total 'Gotras' are recognised within the Maithil Brahmin 
community?
$20$ Gotras.
677
What is a 'Biji Purusha' in the context of Panji genealogy?
The earliest known progenitor or founding ancestor of a specific 
lineage.
678
How many 'Mula Grams' (root villages) were initially identified in 
the system?
$167$ Mula Grams.
679
Identify the $35$th Mula added later to the high-lineage group.
The 'Kharaunm' branch of the 'Phandah' Mula.
680
What defines a 'Tri-pravara' Gotra?
A lineage where three ancestors are credited with composing 
Rigvedic hymns.
681
What defines a 'Pancha-pravara' Gotra?
A lineage where five ancestors are credited with composing 
Rigvedic hymns.
682
Why is marriage prohibited between the Vatsa and Savarna Gotras? Because they share the same set of five 'Pravara' ancestors.
683
Which specific branch of the Yajurveda do the 'Vajasaneyi' 
Brahmins follow?
The Madhyandina branch.
684
Which specific branch of the Samaveda do the 'Chandoga' 
Brahmins follow?
The Kauthuma branch.
685
Who was appointed by King Harisimhadeva to manage the records 
of the Brahmins?
Pandit Gunakar Jha.
686
Who was the first registrar (Panjikar) appointed for the Karna 
Kayasthas?
Shankardatta.
687
Who was the first registrar (Panjikar) appointed for the Kshatriya 
community?
Vijayadatta.
688
What is a 'Siddhanta Patra'?
A formal certificate issued by a Panjikar confirming that a bride 
and groom are not related.
689
Which classification of Maithil Brahmins is traditionally divided 
into eight 'Chaurals' (categories)?
Shrotriya.
690
Which ancient philosopher first mentioned 'Samuha Lekhya' 
(collective genealogical records) in the 'Tantra Varttika'?
Kumarila Bhatta.

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
691
What is the purpose of the 'Dushan Panji'?
To record any known flaws or 'kshana' (blemishes) within a 
particular lineage.
692
What information is primarily tracked in a 'Sakha Panji'?
The branching out of lineages from their original root villages 
(Mula).
693
What does the term 'Asvajan Patra' signify?
A document certifying that the parties are not 'Svapinda' (closely 
related by blood).
694
How many 'Chhadwidh' (checkpoints) are analysed to determine 
marriage eligibility?
Sixteen genealogical points.
695
The five Northern Brahmin groups, including Maithils, are 
collectively known by what name?
Panch Gauda.
696
What is the significance of the year $1760$ AD in Panji history?
Maharaja Madhava Singh initiated the 'Sakha Panji' to further 
categorise lineages.
697
What linguistic script is traditionally used for writing Panji records? Tirhuta (Mithilakshar).
698
Which Gotra is the only one recorded for the Karna Kayastha 
community in the source material?
Kashyapa.
699
In the context of lineage, what does 'Mula' represent?
The original village or 'root' location where the Biji Purusha resided.
700
What rule governs the storage of Panji books to prevent damage?
They must be protected from water, oil, and loose binding.
701
During which month of the year are Panji records traditionally dried 
in the sun?
The month when the Sun enters Leo (approx. $16$ August to $15$ 
September).
702
The first 'Chhadwidh' checkpoint (Pratham Chhadwidh) refers to 
which relative of the bride?
The paternal great-great-grandfather (Vriddha Prapitamaha).
703
Which tier of Maithil Brahmins contains $153$ registered Panjis?
Yogya and Panjibaddha categories.
704
What was the occupation of Chaitanya Mahaprabhu's guru, 
Ramapati Upadhyaya, according to the Panji?
He was a Maithil Brahmin of the 'Karamhe' root village (Mula).
705
Which king of Mithila is mentioned as the 'Chudamani' of the 
Karnata dynasty?
Maharaja Harisimhadeva.
706
The term 'Panchaprastha' refers to which specific group in the Panji? A specific settlement or group within the Keutiram branch.
707
In the Panji terminology, what is the role of a 'Panjikar'?
A specialised priest or scholar who verifies and maintains 
genealogical records.
708
How did the Panji records handle children born to 'Devadasis' in 
historical Mithila?
They were recorded in the 'Dushan Panji' but their descendants 
often remained part of the social fabric.
709
Which document tracks the educational and academic titles 
(Upadhis) of ancestors from $450$ AD onwards?
The ancient Panji Prabandh records.
710
According to the source, why did King Harisimhadeva take the 
Panji records from individuals and assign them to scholars?
Because common people and even some pundits had begun to 
forget their precise lineages, leading to illegal marriages.
711
Which Gotra includes the Mula 'Sakhuri' and ancestors like 
'Mahamaho Deyi'?
Shandilya Gotra.
712
The 'Vatsa' Gotra is associated with which set of five Pravaras?
Aurva, Chyavana, Bhargava, Jamadagnya, and Apnuvana.
713
The 'Kashyapa' Gotra is associated with which three Pravaras?
Kashyapa, Avatsara, and Naidhruva.
714
Which specific Panji record is used to ensure blood-related 
individuals do not marry within 'Sapinda' limits?
Udedh Panji.
715
Which historical figure's lineage in the Panji shows an unbroken 
record for $19$ generations up to the modern era?
The family of Panjikar Modanand Jha.
716
What is the cultural significance of the 'Tirhuta' script according to 
the text?
It is the 'soul' or 'life-force' of the Panji Prabandh.
717
The term 'Svarnav' is a synonym for which Gotra in the Panji 
system?
Savarna Gotra.
718
Which ancestor of the 'Mandi' Mula is cited as a 'Biji Purusha' with 
records dating back to $400$ AD?
Mahamaho Trivenibhata.
719
What major change occurred in the Panji system during the reign of 
Maharaja Madhava Singh?
The creation of hierarchical sub-divisions and the formal 'Sakha' 
(branch) documentation.
720
How many 'Grama' (villages) are typically mentioned in the 'Patra 
Panji' developed $500$ years ago?
Both the current village of residence and the Mula Grama (root 
village).
721
Which document is used to determine 'Sapinda Nivritti' (freedom 
from prohibited blood relations)?
Udedh Panji.
722
Identify the primary source of 'Dharmashastra' rules used by 
Panjikars to resolve marriage disputes.
Manu Smriti and Yajnavalkya Smriti.
723
In the Panji system, what happens to a lineage if a member marries 
a 'Chandal' or someone from an 'Agyat' (unknown) caste?
The lineage suffers 'Dushan' (corruption) and is moved to the 
Dushan Panji.
724
What is the specific prohibition regarding the daughter of a paternal 
uncle's maternal family?
She is ineligible for marriage if she falls within the prohibited 
generation count of the 'Chhadwidh'.
725
Which Mula is associated with the 'Srotriya' family residing in 
'Sarisab'?
Sarisab Mula (Shandilya Gotra).
726
What is the role of 'Asvajan Patra' during the 'Siddhanta' ceremony? It confirms in writing that the groom and bride have no forbidden 
kinship.

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
727
In what year and era was the 'Panji Prabandh' (genealogical 
management system) of Mithila officially initiated?
It was established in 1326 AD (Saka 1248) under the reign of King 
Harisimhadeva.
728
Which Karnat dynasty king is credited with formalising the Panji 
system in Mithila?
Maharaja Harisimhadeva.
729
What was the primary sociological purpose for establishing the 
Panji Prabandh?
To ensure 'Jati Shuddhi' (caste purity) and prevent prohibited 
degrees of relationship in marriages.
730
The introduction of seven generations of ancestors required for 
marriage eligibility in the Panji system is known as _____.
Udegh
731
According to the Panji system, how many maternal and paternal 
generations must be avoided to qualify for a 'Shrotriya' marriage?
Five generations on the mother's side and seven generations on the 
father's side.
732
Name the three primary social categories created by the Panji 
classification of Maithil Brahmins.
Shrotriya, Yogya, and Panjibaddh.
733
How many total 'Gotras' (clans) are documented within the Maithil 
Brahmin community?
20
734
What does the term 'Moolagrama' refer to in the context of Mithila 
genealogy?
The original village or place of residence associated with an 
ancestral lineage.
735
Who was the first 'Panjikar' (genealogist) appointed for the Maithil 
Brahmins by King Harisimhadeva?
Pandit Gunakar Jha of the Pandua Moola.
736
In the Karna Kayastha community, how many 'Gotras' are 
recognized according to the Panji theory?
Only one: the Kashyap Gotra.
737
What is a 'Siddhanta Patra' (also called 'Asvajana Patra') in the 
Panji system?
A formal certificate issued by a Panjikar confirming that a bride 
and groom are not prohibited relatives.
738
Which two Vedic branches (Shakhas) do Maithil Brahmins 
primarily follow?
The Madhyandina Shakha of the Yajurveda and the Kauthuma 
Shakha of the Samaveda.
739
According to the Panji system, a person is ineligible for marriage if 
they are a _____ (related to the same sapinda).
Svajana
740
What is the difference between 'Tri-pravara' and 'Panch-pravara' 
lineages?
Tri-pravara lineages claim three sage ancestors, while Panch-
pravara claim five.
741
How many 'Chhatth' (specific genealogical nodes) are checked to 
determine marriage eligibility?
16
742
Which famous Maithili poet's genealogy is recorded in the Panji, 
showing his descent from Mahamahopadhyaya Jayaditya?
Vidyapati Thakur.
743
The practice of checking 32 specific ancestral lineages for a 
marriage match is called _____.
Battis Moola Ka Udegh
744
Why is the marriage between members of the 'Vatsa' and 'Savarna' 
gotras prohibited in Mithila?
Because they are considered to share the same ancestors (Anya-
onya).
745
What is the 'Dushana Panji'?
A record of perceived flaws or deviations in a lineage's history that 
could affect social standing.
746
In the 18th century, which Maharaja of Darbhanga introduced 
'Sakha Panji' to further classify the Brahmin sub-groups?
Maharaja Madhav Singh.
747
Concept: Panch-Gaur Brahmins
Definition: The five northern Brahmin groups, including Utkal, 
Kanyakubja, Gaur, Maithil, and Saraswat.
748
How many main 'Moolas' were initially established during the Panji 
Prabandh?
167
749
What is the role of a 'Panjikar' in modern Mithila society?
To maintain genealogical records and verify marriage eligibility 
based on ancient texts and rules.
750
True or False: The Panji system was originally used by Brahmins, 
Kayasthas, and Kshatriyas in Mithila.
True.
751
The historical document 'Tantra Varttika' by _____ mentions 
'Samuha Lekhya', an early form of genealogical records.
Kumarila Bhatta
752
According to Manu and Yajnavalkya, a marriage is prohibited if the 
bride is a _____ of the groom's mother.
Sapinda
753
How does the Panji system define the 'First Chhatth' for marriage 
eligibility check?
The paternal great-great-grandfather of the bride/groom.
754
Lineages that did not get recorded in the initial Panji registration 
became known as _____.
Jaibar (or Jaywar)
755
Which script was traditionally used to record and maintain the Panji 
manuscripts?
Tirhuta (Mithilakshar).
756
The genealogical data mapping from 450 AD to 2009 AD is 
metaphorically referred to in the text as _____.
Genome Mapping
757
Which Moola did the famous poet Vidyapati belong to?
Bisaphi (associated with the Kashyap Gotra).
758
Under the Panji rules, can a groom marry his maternal uncle's 
daughter?
No, it is strictly prohibited as per the rule against marrying 
offspring of the mother's brother.
759
What is the 'Laukika' in the context of the Shrotriya Panji?
The documented lineage and social standing specific to the 
Shrotriya category.
760
How many primary 'Moolas' are identified as the most prominent 
among the 167 documented?
34 (later 35 including the Phandah branch).
761
In the process of 'Siddhanta', what is verified if a person is found to 
be in the 'Chhatth' (sixth position) of a lineage?
They are ineligible for marriage if the connection is on the paternal 
side.

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
762
Which specific Gotra is associated with the 'Sandilya' lineage in the 
text?
Sandilya (one of the seven primary gotras).
763
The ancestral lineages that claim descent from the sage 'Kashyap' 
belong to which Gotra?
Kashyap Gotra.
764
What is the significance of the year 1326 AD in Mithila's social 
history?
It marks the formalisation of the Panji Prabandh by Maharaja 
Harisimhadeva.
765
What is the primary difference in the marriage rituals (Upayana) of 
'Chhandoga' (Sama-vedi) and 'Vajasaneyi' (Yajur-vedi) Brahmins?
Chhandoga has four primary samskaras, while Vajasaneyi includes 
Vedarmbha as a distinct fifth samskara.
766
Which Panji type records the 'kshana' (flaws or social falls) in a 
family tree?
Dushana Panji.
767
The Panji records were historically preserved on which material to 
prevent damage from water and oil?
Palm leaves (Talpatra) or Basaha-patra.
768
How many lineages (Sakhas) does the 'Mandara' Moola (Kashyap 
Gotra) have according to the text?
74
769
In the classification of Moolas, names like 'Khadaure', 'Khauade', 
and 'Budhabade' belong to which category?
Ayanth Shrestha (First Category).
770
What is 'Pachhwari-pa' in the context of Maithil Brahmin hierarchy? It refers to the Brahmin lineages remaining outside the Shrotriya 
(Purwari-pa) category.
771
According to the Panji theory, a child born from a 'Svajana' 
(prohibited) marriage is traditionally labelled as _____.
Chandala (in a ritual/social sense)
772
What is the function of the 'Moola Panji'?
It lists all the Gotras and their corresponding ancient Moolas.
773
Which scholar's lineage is traced back 12 generations before him to 
the 'Mandara' Moola in the text?
Mahamahopadhyaya Halayudha.
774
What is the rule for the 'Fifth Chhatth' in checking marriage 
compatibility?
It involves checking the paternal great-grandfather of the bride's 
paternal grandmother.
775
Why did Maharaja Madhav Singh initiate the 'Sakha Panji'?
To establish a more rigid social hierarchy and classification based 
on internal lineage purity.
776
Which Brahmin groups are categorized under the 'Panch Dravida' 
division?
Karnata, Gurjara, Tailanga, Dravida, and Maharashtriya.
777
How many 'Udeghs' (lineage tracks) are found in the Shrotriya 
Panji?
32
778
True or False: The Panji system allowed for the inclusion of 
lineages where a widow's child (after re-marriage) was born.
False; such cases were often recorded as 'Dushana' (flaws) or 
excluded from high ranks.
779
The term for a lineage that has 'fallen' due to improper marriage or 
conduct in the Panji is _____.
Kshana
780
What was the role of 'Shankaradatta' during the reign of 
Harisimhadeva?
He was the first Panjikar appointed for the Karna Kayastha 
community.
781
According to Panji logic, marriage eligibility is regained after how 
many generations of 'Sapinda' separation on the father's side?
Seven (or six in some modified rules).
782
Which script's preservation is closely tied to the survival of the 
Panji records?
Tirhuta script.
783
The term 'Jaywar' refers to Brahmins who are _____.
Not officially registered in the formal Panji hierarchy (Panjibaddh).
784
In the context of Panji, what does 'Ayanth' signify?
A high-ranking or 'excellent' classification of a Moola.
785
Which King's court first debated the issue of 'Svajana' marriage, 
leading to the Panji system?
Maharaja Harisimhadeva, following a controversy involving Pandit 
Harinath Upadhyaya.
786
During which historical period was the 'Panji Prabandh' system for 
Maithil Brahmins, Kayasthas, and Kshatriyas created?
The Late Medieval period.
787
What was the primary purpose of the 'Panji Prabandh' system in 
Mithila?
To ensure 'Jati Shuddha' or caste purity.
788
The term _____ refers to the detailed introduction of $32$ specific 
ancestors required to establish a Brahmin's lineage purity.
Udhedh
789
Which historical ruler is credited with the formal initiation and 
inspiration of the Panji Prabandh in $1326$ AD?
Maharaja Harisimhadeva.
790
In the context of Panji lineage, what is a 'Biji Purush'?
The earliest known ancestor from whom a specific lineage is traced.
791
How many total 'Gotras' (clans) are identified among Maithil 
Brahmins in the Panji records?
$20$
792
List the three main hierarchical categories of Maithil Brahmins 
established during the Panji division.
Shrotriya, Yogya, and Panjibaddha.
793
What term is used for those who were not formally recorded in the 
Panji system but belong to the community?
Jaybar
794
According to the marriage rules for 'Shrotriyas', how many 
generations must be avoided on the father's side?
$7$ generations.
795
According to the marriage rules for 'Shrotriyas', how many 
generations must be avoided on the mother's side?
$5$ generations.

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
796
Panjibaddha Brahmins, also known as lineage descendants, must 
avoid $5$ generations on the maternal side and _____ on the 
paternal side for marriage.
$6$ generations
797
The two main Vedic divisions of Maithil Brahmins are the 
'Samavedi' (Chhandogya) and the 'Shukla Yajurvedi', known as 
_____.
Vajasaneyi
798
What is the total number of 'Mula' (original roots) established in the 
Panji Prabandh?
$167$
799
How many 'Mula Gram' (original villages) were designated as the 
primary roots out of the $167$ total?
$34$ (later $35$ with the addition of the 'Khama' branch).
800
Define 'Tripravara' in the context of Maithil Brahmin lineages.
A lineage whose ancestors composed three specific hymns in the 
Rigveda.
801
Define 'Panchapravara' in the context of Maithil Brahmin lineages.
A lineage whose ancestors composed five specific hymns in the 
Rigveda.
802
Which three Gotras are specifically categorized as 'Panchapravara'?
Vatsa, Savarna, and Bharve.
803
Why is marriage prohibited between the 'Vatsa' and 'Savarna' 
Gotras despite being distinct?
Because they share the same ancient ancestors.
804
Into how many broad regional groups are Indian Brahmins 
traditionally divided in the Puranic texts?
$10$ groups ($5$ Pancha Gauda and $5$ Pancha Dravida).
805
Which regional group of the 'Pancha Gauda' division do the Maithil 
Brahmins belong to?
Maithil.
806
What geographical feature acts as the boundary between the 
'Pancha Gauda' and 'Pancha Dravida' Brahmin groups?
The Vindhyachal mountain range.
807
The 'Vajasaneyi' (Yajurvedi) marriage ceremony can occur during 
the day or night, while the 'Chhandogya' (Samavedi) ceremony 
traditionally takes place after _____.
Sunset (after seeing the star Arundhati).
808
Concept: Sapinda
Definition: Relatives who share the same pinda (ancestral 
body/offering), determining marriage eligibility and ritual impurity.
809
According to Smriti texts like Manu and Yajnavalkya, a bride 
should not share the same _____ or Pravara as the groom.
Gotra
810
Who was the 'Mahapandit' whose accidental marriage to a close 
relative (his paternal cousin's daughter) led to the formalisation of 
Panji records?
Pandit Harinath Upadhyay.
811
What is the 'Siddhanta Patra' (also called 'Asvajana Patra')?
A formal certificate issued by a Panjikar confirming that a bride 
and groom are not ineligible relatives.
812
The official scholar appointed by the King to maintain and verify 
genealogical records is called a _____.
Panjikar
813
In the Panji system, what does the term 'Mula Gram' represent?
The original village or homestead of a family's earliest recorded 
ancestor.
814
How many 'Shrotriya' Panjis are currently maintained?
$32$
815
How many 'Yogya' and 'Panjibaddha' Panjis are currently 
maintained?
$153$
816
The term _____ refers to the specific calculation used to identify 
$16$ critical points in a lineage where a person is in the 'sixth' 
position.
Chhatva
817
Under the 'Chhatva' rule, if any of the $16$ identified ancestors 
appear in the groom's paternal side within $6$ generations, is the 
marriage allowed?
No, it is prohibited.
818
What is the 'Dushan Panji'?
A record detailing 'faults' or historical blemishes within specific 
lineages.
819
Which script is traditionally used to write and preserve Panji 
records?
Tirhuta (Mithilakshar).
820
The first official Panjikar for Maithil Brahmins appointed by 
Harisimhadeva was _____.
Gunakar Jha.
821
Who was appointed as the first Panjikar for the 'Karna Kayasthas'?
Shankardatta.
822
The Karna Kayasthas are generally associated with which single 
Gotra?
Kashyapa.
823
According to the texts, during which period (months) should Panji 
books be dried in the sun to prevent damage?
When the Sun is in the Leo (Simha) rashi (approx. $16$ August to 
$15$ September).
824
What is a 'Laukika' in the context of Panji classification?
The specific lineage branch or social rank within the Shrotriya 
category.
825
The 'Shrotriya' Brahmins are sometimes referred to as 'Pubari' 
(Easterners), while others are called _____ (Westerners).
Pachhabari
826
Which type of Panji record tracks changes in residence and family 
expansion over the last $500$ years?
Pan Panji.
827
The historical figure _____ is recorded in the Panji as having the 
lineage title 'Kavishekhar' and serving in the Nepal court.
Jyotirishwar Thakur.

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
828
Which famous saint's guru, Ishwar Puri, is linked to the 'Katih' 
Mula of the Maithil Brahmins in the records?
Chaitanya Mahaprabhu.
829
The ancient mathematician _____ is mentioned in the Panji lineage 
under the Mandaar Mula.
Aryabhata
830
What is the primary Vedic branch followed by 'Chhandogya' 
Brahmins?
Samaveda (Kauthuma branch).
831
What is the primary Vedic branch followed by 'Vajasaneyi' 
Brahmins?
Yajurveda (Madhyandina branch).
832
The concept of 'Ayanati Shrestha' refers to which category of Mula? The first/highest category of original lineages (e.g. Khadaure, 
Khauale).
833
How many ancestors are included in a standard 'Udhedh' for 
marriage verification?
$32$ ancestors (covering paternal and maternal lines).
834
The scholar _____ is credited with establishing the rule that the 
$7^{th}$ generation on the paternal side is eligible for marriage.
Maharaja Mahesh Thakur.
835
The term 'Svarnakarnaka' refers to what practice regarding Panji 
maintenance?
Digital imaging or copying of the Tirhuta script into modern 
Devanagari/digital formats.
836
What is the primary objective of the 'Panji Prabandh' system in 
Mithila?
Maintaining 'Jati Shuddhata' (caste purity) and genealogical records.
837
The Panji system was established to regulate lineages for which 
three social classes?
Brahmins, Kayasthas, and Kshatriyas.
838
In the context of Panji, what does the term 'Udegh' signify?
The introduction of 32 ancestral lineages required to verify marriage 
eligibility.
839
How many maternal generations must be avoided for a marriage to 
be considered valid under the Shrotriya rules?
Five generations.
840
How many paternal generations must be avoided for a marriage to 
be considered valid under the Shrotriya rules?
Seven generations.
841
Which specific ancestor count defines the 'Udegh' of a family?
A total of thirty-two ancestors.
842
Name the four hierarchical categories of Maithil Brahmins 
established by the Panji system.
Shrotriya, Yogya, Panjibaddha, and Jayabar.
843
What defines the 'Jayabar' category of Maithil Brahmins?
Those who were not formally registered in the Panji records.
844
How many distinct 'Gotras' (clans) are identified in the Maithil 
Brahmin community?
Twenty.
845
How many 'Moolas' (original ancestral villages) were identified 
during the system's inception?
One hundred and sixty-seven.
846
Out of the 167 Moolas, how many are considered 'Main' or 
'Mukhya' Moolas?
Thirty-four or thirty-five.
847
What is the difference between 'Tri-pravara' and 'Pancha-pravara'?
The number of ancestors (three or five) who were Rigvedic hymn 
composers.
848
Which two major Vedic branches are followed by Maithil 
Brahmins?
Samaveda and Yajurveda.
849
The Yajurvedi Maithil Brahmins follow which specific Shakha?
Madhyandina (Vajasaneyi).
850
The Samavedi Maithil Brahmins follow which specific Shakha?
Kauthuma (Chhandogya).
851
Which geographical boundary divides the 'Pancha Gauda' from the 
'Pancha Dravida' Brahmins?
The Vindhyachal Mountains.
852
Maithil Brahmins belong to which broader regional grouping of 
North Indian Brahmins?
Pancha Gauda.
853
Who was the Karnat dynasty king that formalised the Panji system?
Maharaja Harisimhadeva.
854
In which year (AD) was the Mithila Panji system officially 
consolidated?
1326 AD (Saka 1248).
855
What prompted the creation of the formal Panji system in the 14th 
century?
An accidental incestuous marriage involving Pandit Harinath 
Upadhyaya due to ignorance of lineage.
856
What is 'Siddhanta' (or Siddhanta Patra) in Maithil marriage 
traditions?
A formal permit issued by a Panjikar certifying that the couple is 
not blood-related.
857
Who is a 'Panjikar'?
A genealogical expert appointed to maintain records and verify 
marriage eligibility.
858
Name the first Panjikar appointed for Maithil Brahmins.
Gunakar Jha.
859
Name the first Panjikar appointed for the Karna Kayastha 
community.
Shankardatta.
860
What is a 'Beeji Purusha'?
The earliest known progenitor or root ancestor of a specific lineage 
recorded in the Panji.
861
Explain the concept of 'Moola Gram'.
The original village associated with the Beeji Purusha of a family 
line.
862
According to the Panji system, what occurs if a person marries a 
'Svajana' (close relative)?
The offspring is traditionally considered 'Chandala' or socially 
excluded from Aryan status.
863
Which philosopher's work, 'Tantra Vartika', provided early 7th-
century evidence for maintaining lineage records?
Kumarila Bhatta.

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
864
The 'Shakha Panji' (branch records) were introduced during the 
reign of which king?
Maharaja Madhav Singh.
865
What does 'Dushana Panji' record?
Defects, violations of caste rules, or historical flaws in a family's 
lineage.
866
In modern terms, what is the Panji system compared to by the 
authors?
Genome Mapping or Chromosome identification.
867
How many 'Chhadvis' (ancestral checkpoints) are tracked to 
determine marriage eligibility?
Sixteen.
868
A marriage is prohibited if any of the 16 'Chhadvis' of the bride 
appear in the _____ of the groom.
Paternal lineage (Pitripaksha).
869
What is the rule regarding the Gotra of the bride and groom?
They must not belong to the same Gotra (Sagotra marriage is 
forbidden).
870
Is a marriage between two people of the same Pravara permitted?
No, they must have distinct Pravaras.
871
What is the prohibition regarding the bride and the groom's 
maternal uncle?
The groom cannot marry the daughter of his maternal uncle.
872
How many Gotras are considered the most prominent or 
'Mahakulas' in Maithil society?
Seven (Shandilya, Vatsa, Savarna, Kashyapa, Parashara, 
Bharadwaja, and Katyayana).
873
What is the significance of the 'Saka 1248' date in Mithila history?
It marks the formal registration and inception of the Panji records.
874
Which hierarchical category is considered the highest in the Maithil 
Brahmin social order?
Shrotriya.
875
The term 'Laukika' refers to which type of Panji specifically?
The Shrotriya Panji.
876
Under which conditions can the Panji books be dried to ensure 
preservation?
When the sun is in the Leo (Simha) constellation (approx. August-
September).
877
What three things must Panji books be protected from according to 
traditional verses?
Water (Jala), Oil (Taila), and loose binding (Shithila Bandhan).
878
How many hierarchical 'Chauwalas' (classes) exist within the 
Shrotriya Panji?
Eight.
879
What is the 'Asvajana Patra'?
A document proving that the bride and groom are not related by 
blood within prohibited degrees.
880
Which Panji type tracks descendants and branch-wise expansions 
of a family?
Shakha Panji.
881
What are 'Aandhi' Moolas?
Main ancestral lineages from which various sub-branches 
(Shakhas) emerge.
882
Followers of the Kauthuma Shakha are specifically known as _____. Chhandoga.
883
Followers of the Madhyandina Shakha are specifically known as 
_____.
Vajasaneyi.
884
What is the relationship between the 'Vatsa' and 'Savarna' Gotras 
regarding marriage?
Marriage between them is forbidden because they share the same 
ancestors.
885
Which Gotra is associated with the 'Mandi' village and has 74 sub-
branches?
Kashyapa.
886
Which category of Brahmins is referred to as 'Pachhwari-paat'?
The non-Shrotriya Brahmins (Yogya and Panjibaddha).
887
The 'Adho-gami' nature of Panji refers to what social phenomenon?
The decline in status resulting from marriage into lower hierarchical 
ranks.
888
Who was the 'Beeji Purusha' of the Simhashram Moola according 
to the text?
Mahamahopadhyaya Halayudha.
889
In Karna Kayasthas, how many Gotras are typically recognised?
One (Kashyapa).
890
What does 'Sapiá¹‡á¸ a' refer to in the context of marriage laws?
Sharing the same body particles or having common ancestors 
within defined generations.
891
Who was the first Kshatriya Panjikar appointed by King 
Harisimhadeva?
Vijayadatta.
892
What distinguishes 'Tri-pravara' Gotras from 'Panch-pravara' Gotras? Tri-pravara Gotras cite three sages in their ritual invocation; Panch-
pravara cite five.
893
List three Gotras that fall under the 'Panch-pravara' category.
Vatsa, Savarna, and Garga.
894
Name the first 'Chhadvi' (ancestor) checked for a bride's eligibility.
The paternal great-great-grandfather (Vriddha Prapitamaha).
895
What role does the 'Dushana Panji' play in historical research?
It provides evidence of historical social anomalies, such as inter-
caste marriages or widow remarriages.
896
Which kingâ€™s assembly appointed Gunakar Jha as the head of 
Panji Prabandh?
Maharaja Harisimhadeva.
897
What is the 'Prathama Chhadvi' in the verification process?
The bride's paternal great-great-grandfather.
898
Marriage with a 'Matama-gotra' (mother's gotra) is prohibited if the 
groom is within which generation?
Within seven generations on the father's side and five on the 
mother's side.
899
What does the text claim is the 'Pranadhara' (life force) of the 
Tirhuta script?
The Panji Prabandh records.
900
In the Panji terminology, what is a 'Kula'?
A lineage or family group sharing common heritage.

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
901
Why is the Maithil Panji system considered 'unparalleled' by the 
authors?
It contains continuous genealogical records for over 1000 years 
across an entire community.
902
Who was the author of the 'Smritisara', whose marriage mistake led 
to the Panji system?
Pandit Harinath Upadhyaya.
903
What is 'Pancha Gauda'?
The five regional Brahmin groups of North India: Saraswat, 
Kanyakubja, Gauda, Maithil, and Utkal.
904
What is 'Pancha Dravida'?
The five regional Brahmin groups of South India: Karnataka, 
Gurjara, Tailanga, Dravida, and Maharashtra.
905
Which Gotra includes sages Shandilya, Asita, and Devala in its 
Pravara?
Shandilya.
906
The 'Vajasaneyi' marriage ceremony can occur at what time?
During the day or night, as there is no specific restriction mentioned 
compared to Chhandoga.
907
The 'Chhandoga' marriage ceremony must occur after which event?
After sunset, following the sighting of the star Arundhati.
908
What does the 'Moola Panji' specifically track?
The original ancestral identity and village of a family.
909
The 'Genome Mapping' of Mithila lineages described in the text 
spans from _____ AD to 2009 AD.
450
910
Term: Udhredh
Definition: The formal introduction or record of seven generations 
of ancestors used to determine marriage eligibility.
911
In the Panji system, how many total ancestors (kula) are 
traditionally identified in a full 'udhredh' introduction?
32
912
Name the three main hierarchical categories of Maithil Brahmins 
created through the Panji system.
Shrotriya, Yogya, and Panjibaddh.
913
What term is used for those who were not formally recorded in the 
Panji system?
Jaywar
914
According to traditional Shrotriya rules, how many generations 
must be avoided for marriage on the maternal side?
Five generations.
915
According to traditional Shrotriya rules, how many generations 
must be avoided for marriage on the paternal side?
Seven generations.
916
How many 'Gotras' are formally recognised within the Maithil 
Brahmin community?
20
917
How many 'Mula Grams' (original villages) were initially identified 
during the Panji classification?
167
918
Out of the 167 original villages, how many are considered the 'Main 
Mulas' (primary roots)?
34 (later expanded to 35).
919
Which sage-based lineages belong to the 'Panch-pravara' (five-
ancestor) category?
Vatsa, Savarna, and Bharva.
920
Define the term 'Tri-pravara' in the context of Maithil Gotras.
Lineages whose three ancestors were composers of Vedic hymns 
(suktas).
921
Why is marriage prohibited between the Vatsa and Savarna Gotras 
despite being separate names?
Because they share the same common ancestors (identical Pravaras).
922
According to the 'Pancha Gauda' classification, which five Brahmin 
groups reside north of the Vindhya mountains?
Utkala, Kanyakubja, Gauda, Maithila, and Saraswata.
923
Which historical region's capital was the original home of the 
Maithil Brahmins?
Videha
924
Maithil Brahmins follow which specific branch (shakha) of the 
Yajurveda?
Madhyandina
925
What name is given to Maithil Brahmins who follow the Kauthuma 
branch of the Samaveda?
Chandoga
926
Maithil Brahmins who follow the Madhyandina branch of the 
Yajurveda are known as _____.
Vajasaneyi
927
How does the 'Uttara Vivaha' of Samavedic (Chandoga) Brahmins 
differ from Yajurvedic Brahmins?
It must take place after sunset following the viewing of the 
Arundhati star.
928
The first reference to 'Samuha Lekhya' (collective lineage records) 
is found in which philosopher's work?
Kumarila Bhatta (in the Tantravarttika).
929
Which king of the Karnata dynasty is credited with the formalised 
'Panji Prabandh' in 1326 AD?
Maharaja Harisimhadeva
930
What was the specific year in the Shaka calendar when the Panji 
system was established?
Shaka 1248
931
Who was the scholar appointed by Maharaja Harisimhadeva to 
organise the Maithil Brahmin Panji?
Saduphadhyaya Gunakar Jha
932
The certificate issued by a Panjikar confirming that a bride and 
groom are not related (non-sapinda) is called the _____.
Siddhanta Patra (or Asvajanapa Patra)
933
The scholar appointed for the Panji management of Karna 
Kayasthas was _____.
Shankar Datta
934
The scholar appointed for the Panji management of the Kshatriya 
community was _____.
Vijay Datta
935
Under whose reign was the 'Shakha Panji' (branch records) 
initiated, leading to modern social divisions?
Maharaja Madhav Singh

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
936
How many Gotras are identified for Karna Kayasthas in the Panji 
system?
One (Kashyap).
937
In the preservation of Panji manuscripts, during which month 
should the books be dried in the sun?
When the Sun is in the zodiac of Leo (approx. 16th August to 15th 
September).
938
Marriage is prohibited if either the bride or groom falls into the 
_____ position (Chhatth) of the other's lineage.
Sixth
939
M.M. Maharaja Mahesh Thakur ruled that the _____ generation of 
the paternal side is acceptable for marriage.
Seventh
940
The primary language and script used for the original Panji records 
is _____.
Tirhuta (Mithilakshar)
941
Concept: Laukik
Definition: The social status or 'honour' level recorded within the 
Shrotriya Panji branches.
942
In the calculation of marriage eligibility, how many specific 
positions (Chhatth) are verified in the bride's lineage?
16 positions.
943
Which Gotra does the scholar Gunakar Jha (the first Brahmin 
Panjikar) belong to?
Sandilya (specifically the Mohi-ji branch).
944
What is the consequence of marrying a 'Svajana' (prohibited 
relative) according to the Smritis mentioned in the text?
The offspring are considered 'Chandala' and the parents are 
ostracised from the Arya dignity.
945
The term 'Pubaripaha' refers to which category of Maithil Brahmins? Shrotriya Brahmins.
946
The term 'Pachhbaripaha' refers to which category of Maithil 
Brahmins?
Jaywar Brahmins (non-Shrotriya).
947
Which type of Panji documents the 'Sapinda' (blood relation) details 
and ancestral names?
Udhredh Panji
948
The text lists _____ as a famous historical figure whose guru, 
Iban/Pratitidatta, is recorded in the Panji.
Chaitanya Mahaprabhu
949
Term: Pancha Dravida
Definition: The five Brahmin groups residing south of the Vindhya 
mountains (Karnat, Gurjar, Tailang, Dravid, and Maharashtra).
950
According to the text, a student of Panji Shastra must avoid _____ 
when handling manuscripts to prevent decay.
Water, oil, and loose binding.
951
The scholar _____ is mentioned as having identified 4,000 Gotras 
in his historical research.
M.P. Chichalal Rao
952
Which Gotra is associated with the Pravaras: Kashyap, Avatsar, and 
Naidhruva?
Kashyap
953
What is the primary purpose of the 'Panji Prabandh' in the Mithila 
region?
To maintain caste purity and determine marriage eligibility for 
Brahmins, Kayasthas, and Kshatriyas.
954
In what year was the formal Panji system established under the 
patronage of King Harisimhadeva?
1326 AD (Saka 1248).
955
Who was the Maithil Brahmin appointed by King Harisimhadeva to 
manage the Brahmin Panji records?
Gunakar Jha.
956
The term 'Uteá¸ h' refers to the introduction and lineage of how 
many ancestors required for marriage verification?
Thirty-two ancestors (Battiis Kul).
957
Identify the three main social tiers of Maithil Brahmins established 
during the Panji management.
Srotriya, Yogya, and Panjibaddh.
958
What is the common name for Brahmins who were not formally 
recorded in the Panji system?
Jaebaar (or Jayabaar).
959
According to the Panji system, how many generations must be 
avoided for marriage on the father's side?
Seven generations.
960
According to the Panji system, how many generations must be 
avoided for marriage on the mother's side?
Five generations.
961
The Panji records identify a total of how many distinct 'Gotras' 
(clans) among Maithil Brahmins?
Twenty.
962
How many 'MÅ«l Grams' (original villages) were initially identified 
in the Panji system?
One hundred and sixty-seven.
963
Out of the 167 original villages, how many were designated as the 
'Main MÅ«ls' (Mukhya MÅ«l)?
Thirty-four (later thirty-five including the Khanam branch of 
Phandah).
964
The term 'Beeji Purush' refers to which individual in a lineage?
The earliest known ancestor or the progenitor of a specific 
clan/branch.
965
What defines the 'Tri-Pravara' category in the Panji system?
Clans whose three ancestors were composers of Rigvedic hymns.
966
Which three Maithil Brahmin Gotras are classified as 'Panch-
Pravara'?
Vatsa, Savarna, and Bharadwaj (though the text lists 
Bhargava/Vatsa/Savarna variants).
967
Name the two Vedic branches followed by Maithil Brahmins.
Yajurvedic (Madhyandini branch) and Samavedic (Kauthum 
branch).
968
What is the title given to Maithil Brahmins who follow the 
Samavedic Kauthum branch?
Chhandogya.
969
What is the title given to Maithil Brahmins who follow the 
Yajurvedic Madhyandini branch?
Vajasaneyi.
970
Explain the significance of the document known as 'Siddhanta 
Patra' (or Ashvajan Patra).
A formal certificate issued by a Panjikar confirming that the bride 
and groom are not related within forbidden degrees.

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
971
The 'Pancha Gauda' classification of Brahmins includes which five 
regional groups?
Utkala, Kanyakubja, Gauda, Maithil, and Saraswat.
972
The 'Pancha Dravida' Brahmins are geographically located in which 
direction relative to the Vindhyachal mountains?
South.
973
Why is marriage traditionally forbidden between the Savarna and 
Vatsa Gotras in the Panji system?
Because they share the same ancestors (lineage) despite having 
different clan names.
974
What is the specific duty of a 'Panjikar'?
To maintain genealogical records and verify the legality of 
marriages based on lineage.
975
In the context of Panji, what does the term 'Chhadvith' (or Chhad-
vrit) represent?
The sixteen specific forbidden relations or 'checkpoints' in the 
lineage of the bride and groom.
976
What is the first 'Chhadvith' rule regarding the bride's lineage?
The relationship to the bride's paternal great-great-grandfather.
977
Identify the 'Main MÅ«l' associated with the Sandilya Gotra that 
has many branches.
Digho (or Dighve).
978
Which scholar's 7th-century work 'Tantra Varttika' mentions the 
'Samuh Lekhya' as a precursor to the Panji system?
Kumarila Bhatta.
979
The 'Dushana Panji' records what specific type of information?
Flaws or incidents of 'impurity' within a lineage to prevent their 
recurrence.
980
What is the difference between marriage rituals for Chhandogya 
and Vajasaneyi Brahmins regarding the 'Tara' (stars)?
Chhandogya marriages involve viewing the Arundhati star after 
sunset, while Vajasaneyi marriages have no such requirement.
981
The term 'MÅ«l' in Panji theory primarily designates which two 
identifiers?
Progenitor (Beeji Purush) and Original Village (MÅ«l Gram).
982
How many 'Sakha Panjis' (branch records) were initiated during the 
reign of Maharaja Madhav Singh?
The text notes they began during his reign to manage increasing 
social divisions and hierarchies.
983
Which Gotra is the only one found among the Karna Kayasthas 
according to the source?
Kashyap.
984
What was the 'Siddhanta' requirement introduced to ensure 
scriptural compliance in marriages?
No marriage is considered valid unless verified and approved by an 
appointed Panjikar.
985
The classification 'Pachhwari Paat' refers to which group of Maithil 
Brahmins?
The non-Srotriya Brahmins (Yogya and Panjibaddh).
986
How many categories of marriage records (Panjis) are mentioned, 
such as MÅ«l, Sakha, and Gotra?
Seven (MÅ«l, Sakha, Gotra, Patra, Dushana, Uteá¸ h, and Ashvajan-
Patra).
987
What unique genealogical document is used to track 'Sapinda' 
(blood relation) limits?
Uteá¸ h Panji.
988
According to the text, what happened to the Panji system for the
